classes.name: BaseModel
classes.description:
Bases: ABC
The base class for all model types.
Construct a new BaseModel from a dictionary of parameters.
value – The value to construct the BaseModel from.
A new BaseModel object, constructed using the given parameters.
XRPLModelException – If the dictionary provided is invalid.
Creates a BaseModel object based on a JSON-like dictionary of keys in the JSON format used by the binary codec, or an actual JSON string representing the same data.
value – The dictionary or JSON string to be instantiated.
A BaseModel object instantiated from the input.
Checks whether the provided dictionary is a dictionary representation of this class.
Note: This only checks the exact model, and does not count model inheritance. This method returns False if the dictionary represents a subclass of this class.
dictionary – The dictionary to check. Note: The input dictionary can be of non-dict type. For instance, a str representation of JSON.
True if dictionary is a dict representation of an instance of this class; False if not.
Returns whether this BaseModel is valid.
Whether this BaseModel is valid.
Returns the dictionary representation of a BaseModel.
If not overridden, returns the object dict with all non-None values.
The dictionary representation of a BaseModel.
Raises if this object is invalid.
XRPLModelException – if this object is invalid.

methods.name: from_dict
methods.signature: classmethod from_dict(value: Dict[str, str | int | List[Any] | Dict[str, Any]]) → Self
methods.description:
Construct a new BaseModel from a dictionary of parameters.
value – The value to construct the BaseModel from.
A new BaseModel object, constructed using the given parameters.
XRPLModelException – If the dictionary provided is invalid.
parameters: value – The value to construct the BaseModel from.
returns: A new BaseModel object, constructed using the given parameters.

methods.name: from_xrpl
methods.signature: classmethod from_xrpl(value: str | Dict[str, Any]) → Self
methods.description:
Creates a BaseModel object based on a JSON-like dictionary of keys in the JSON format used by the binary codec, or an actual JSON string representing the same data.
value – The dictionary or JSON string to be instantiated.
A BaseModel object instantiated from the input.
parameters: value – The dictionary or JSON string to be instantiated.
returns: A BaseModel object instantiated from the input.

methods.name: is_dict_of_model
methods.description:
Checks whether the provided dictionary is a dictionary representation of this class.
Note: This only checks the exact model, and does not count model inheritance. This method returns False if the dictionary represents a subclass of this class.
dictionary – The dictionary to check. Note: The input dictionary can be of non-dict type. For instance, a str representation of JSON.
True if dictionary is a dict representation of an instance of this class; False if not.
parameters: dictionary – The dictionary to check. Note: The input dictionary can be of non-dict type. For instance, a str representation of JSON.
returns: True if dictionary is a dict representation of an instance of this class; False if not.

methods.name: is_valid
methods.description:
Returns whether this BaseModel is valid.
Whether this BaseModel is valid.
returns: Whether this BaseModel is valid.

methods.name: to_dict
methods.description:
Returns the dictionary representation of a BaseModel.
If not overridden, returns the object dict with all non-None values.
The dictionary representation of a BaseModel.
returns: The dictionary representation of a BaseModel.

methods.name: validate
methods.description:
Raises if this object is invalid.
XRPLModelException – if this object is invalid.

classes.name: Response
classes.signature: class xrpl.models.response.Response(*, status: ~xrpl.models.response.ResponseStatus = <object object>, result: ~typing.Dict[str, ~typing.Any] = <object object>, id: int | str | None = None, type: ~xrpl.models.response.ResponseType | None = None)
classes.description:
Bases: BaseModel
The base class for all network response types.
Represents fields common to all response types.
Returns whether the request contains at least one transactions with the partial payment flag set.
True if at least one transaction in this Response has the partial payment flag set. False otherwise.
Returns whether the request was successfully received and understood by the server.
Whether the request was successfully received and understood by the server.
This field is required.
This field is required.

methods.name: contains_partial_payment
methods.description:
Returns whether the request contains at least one transactions with the partial payment flag set.
True if at least one transaction in this Response has the partial payment flag set. False otherwise.
returns: True if at least one transaction in this Response has the partial payment flag set. False otherwise.

methods.name: is_successful
methods.description:
Returns whether the request was successfully received and understood by the server.
Whether the request was successfully received and understood by the server.
returns: Whether the request was successfully received and understood by the server.
